title: intrusive rawptr list: list_remove + container_of
statement: From docs/specpack/idioms.md #6 (intrusive rawptr structures + container_of). Define `struct Link { next: rawptr Link, prev: rawptr Link }` and `struct Node { link: Link, val: i64 }` (the `link` is embedded, not owned separately). Then define two functions: `fn list_remove(n: rawptr Link) -> unit` — unlink node `n` from its doubly-linked list by splicing its neighbors together (`prev.next := n.next`, `next.prev := n.prev`), and `fn node_of(l: rawptr Link) -> rawptr Node` — recover the containing `Node` from a pointer to its embedded `link` field via `offsetof(Node, link)` (container_of). All raw-pointer operations belong inside `unsafe` blocks with real justifications. Define only these items.
